theme: The moment 200 people almost ate dinner in the rain — and what saved it

linkedin.text:
Tuscany. October 2025. 200 senior executives. A 3-course farm-to-table dinner planned on a hilltop vineyard terrace.

At 14:00, the day-of, the forecast flipped. 90% chance of thunderstorms by 18:30. Dinner was scheduled to start at 19:30.

Here's what most agencies do at that moment:
→ Panic email to client
→ Cancel and refund
→ Move dinner to hotel buffet (the client paid €120k to avoid)

Here's what we do:

14:15 — Field call to the vineyard owner. He has a barrel cellar that fits 220 max with a tight squeeze. We pace it: yes, but we need to flip the entire setup in 4 hours.

14:30 — Three calls: catering (can the kitchen flow change?), lighting team (cellar has zero rigging — can we suspend from beams?), local rental (we need 22 round tables delivered by 16:30).

14:45 — All three say yes. We commit. We don't tell the client yet — only if it works.

15:00–18:00 — Full setup teardown. The terrace gets a backup plan only (in case weather flips back). The cellar gets the real event. Candles instead of LEDs (no rigging needed). String quartet repositioned 4 times until acoustic is right.

18:30 — Thunderstorm hits exactly on time.

19:30 — 200 executives walk into a candlelit barrel cellar that looks like it was designed for them three months ago. They never knew there was a plan B.

The client found out in the post-event debrief. The CEO said: "That cellar IS the memory of the trip."

This is what "event production" actually means. Not just executing the plan — having three plans ready, and switching between them invisibly.

Weather is the easy one. Try a flight cancellation 3 hours before arrival. Try a chef who quits day-of. Try a key speaker testing positive for COVID at the airport.

We've seen all of it. The job is to make sure your CEO never has to.

📩 alon@upe.co.il

Alon Ouaknine

#BehindTheScenes #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #UproductionEvents #MICE #EventProduction #EventPlanning #B2B #Tuscany #CrisisManagement
